rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/26/25. The following issues must be be cleared two weeks prior to the date: 1. The following information must be provided by a verified supplement: (1) The date of death and the name of the decedent's parents. LRSF 14.18.D.1) (2) Petition #8 must identify a parent of the petitioner - whether she is a child of three surviving siblings of the decedent or a child of one of them. Each nomination identifies the petitioner as "my niece." 2. Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [JC Form DE-147S] has not been filed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEORGE F THOMAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/24/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration to that effect must be filed, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. [LRSF 14.18 C.1.] (2) Item 8 of the petition must indicate the relationship to the decedent of each person. (3) If a will names a trust or trustee as a devisee, the trustee(s) must be clearly identified in item 8. Where the personal representative and the sole trustee are the same person, or if there is no trustee, the beneficiaries of the trust must be clearly identified. [Probate Code Sect. 1208(b) and LRSF 14.18 C.2.] (4) Item 8 of the petition must include all persons mentioned in the will. (5) The examiner is also aware that a creditor's claim has been filed by Bin Bin Thomas. Her allegation that they were not, in fact, divorced, will need to be addressed. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/10/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
